facts.statement: Removing the context encoder or multi-scale fusion causes the largest AUROC drops, indicating that architectural context integration is the main driver of top-line discrimination.

Removing the calibration loss causes only a modest AUROC drop but the largest increase in calibration error, indicating that this objective contributes more to probability quality than to raw discrimination.

design_notes: Separate primary-metric loss from secondary-metric shifts so discrimination and calibration penalties are not conflated.
Keep the full model in view as the explicit zero-reference anchor.
Encode ablation family and metric identity with different channels so the figure remains decodable without color alone.
